Monitoring & Evaluation
Portal
SDG Health Indicators, Disease Programme Metrics, Health-related Legislations &
Data Source Directory for the Philippines
Old: WHO Philippines —M&E Indicator Portal
New: WHO PHL M&E Portal
What is the Portal?
A centralized, searchable reference tool built for the WHO Philippines Country Office that consolidates all
health M&E indicators, TWGs, health-related legislations, WHO’s support at the subnational level, performance
monitoring, and key indicators –all into one interface.
🔍Smart Search & Filter
search by keyword, filter by data source, programme, or NOH 2023-2028 status
📋Programme Sidebar
quick navigation across health programme areas
🔗Linked Data Sources
hyperlinks to international and local references
🔗Access
fully functional, FREE website (to be transferred to WHO website/WHO IP);
works online and partially offline; in desktop, tablet and mobile/android phone
CHARACTERISTICS:
Key Data Sources
🌐International / WHO Sources
WHO Global Health Observatory (GHO)
SDG indicators, mortality, disease burden
WHO GLASS Report
Antimicrobial resistance surveillance
WHO Global Monitoring Report
UHC Service Coverage Index
WHO Global TB Report
TB mortality & treatment data
🇵🇭 National Sources (Philippines)
FHSIS (Field Health Service Information System)
Primary source for routine facility data
Philippine Health Statistics (DOH)
Vital, natality, morbidity & mortality stats
PSA
NDHS, CRVS, SDG Watch
Others
PhilHealth, NOH 2023-2028, Local Health Scorecard, ITIS, PhilPen,
mHGap
Additional sources: HARP (HIV), GATS, Expanded National Nutrition Survey, World Bank Report
Portal Architecture/Development
Methodology
- UI: HTML + CSS3; Frontend: JS (ES6); Backend: JSON-style Array
Why HTML? Custom HTML vs. Google Sites vs. R Shiny
Feature Custom HTML/JS Google Site R Shiny
Performance Fast. Loads instantly Slow. Highly affected by
internet connection
Average
Deployment Can be hosted on:
-
any web server
-
GitHub Page/s (FREE!) or
-
shared as an offline file
Must be hosted within the
Google ecosystem and
requires Google accounts.
Requires a dedicated Shiny
Server, Posit Connect, or
Shinyapps.io (limited size)

(html language)
No lag experienced
Easier (no coding experience
needed)
